challenges.description: SSL/TLS final_hours math error. base_hours=40 (10 certs x 4hr) with many_sites 1.5x should yield 60hr, but final_hours=30. Unit count already accounts for many sites — multiplier double-counts.
challenges.resolution: Remove many_sites multiplier from ssl_tls — unit count (10 certs) already reflects multi-site. Final_hours should be 40hr (+10hr to total estimate). Multiplier double-counts when applied on top of per-site unit count.

challenges.description: Three components (networking_vnet, cicd_pipeline, monitoring_setup) hit the 50% AI savings cap but ai_alternatives_summary.savings_capped_components is empty. Undisclosed cap application.
challenges.resolution: Document all three components in savings_capped_components. No impact on totals (caps were already applied), but transparency improves credibility.

challenges.description: Azure DMS / MI Link listed as AI alternative. DMS classic retires March 2026. Verify MI Link savings (expected 5hr) for 250GB database.
challenges.resolution: MI Link confirmed as recommended approach — only truly online migration option. Uses Always On technology for near-real-time replication. 5hr expected savings reasonable. Rename to 'MI Link (Managed Instance Link)'.

challenges.description: DNS cutover at 30hrs (5 zones x 4hr x 1.5x many_sites). 30 hours = ~4 engineer-days. Seems inflated for 5 Cloudflare zones.
challenges.resolution: 30hrs for 5-zone multi-site DNS cutover reasonable with TTL management, record migration, validation, rollback planning, and Cloudflare-specific configuration. Critical-path item shouldn't be underestimated.

challenges.description: Analysis challenge CHK-010 recommended adding risk for temporary AWS↔Azure connectivity during migration. Risk not in estimate.
challenges.resolution: ASMP-019 (no VPN, 8hr widening) partially covers hybrid connectivity. Recommend adding explicit risk in next estimate version.

challenges.description: Playwright AI test generation credited with 8hr expected savings. Verify current AI capabilities for Sitecore rendering validation.
challenges.resolution: Playwright Test Agents (new 2025) explore apps and generate test plans via AI. MCP server + Copilot integration. 8hr expected / 3hr pessimistic is reasonable range.

challenges.description: Solr standalone at 38.4hrs with zero AI alternatives. No automation tooling for Solr migration.
challenges.resolution: Solr migration is genuinely manual — no AI tooling for schema review, config migration, or index rebuild. Realistic assessment.

challenges.description: Project management at ~43hrs expected with no multipliers despite multi-site/multi-env complexity.
challenges.resolution: PM is per-engagement overhead, not per-component. 40hr base within typical range. Grows organically if engagement extends.

challenges.description: Cutover execution at 24hrs has no AI alternatives. Azure Migrate or ASR could potentially assist.
challenges.resolution: Cutover is inherently manual — go/no-go decisions, live validation, DNS switches, rollback readiness require human judgment. No appropriate AI alternatives.

challenges.description: Azure Cache for Redis is RETIRING. Premium tier creation blocked for new customers April 2026. Must use Azure Managed Redis instead.
challenges.data_reference: redis_session references Azure Cache for Redis Premium P1
challenges.resolution: MAJOR: Azure Cache for Redis retiring all tiers. Premium creation blocked April 2026, retirement Sept 2028. Must use Azure Managed Redis (Redis Enterprise-based) for 2026 deployment. Sitecore session state compatible (Redis protocol). Update estimate and knowledge files.

challenges.description: RISK-008 CI/CD hours_impact (24h) not reconciled with estimate CI/CD hours (20.8h). Analysis challenge recommended reducing to 16hr.
challenges.resolution: Risk buffer (24hr) and estimate base (20.8hr) serve different purposes — base is expected effort, risk is contingency in pessimistic range. Correctly separated. Recommend aligning RISK-008 to 16hr in future analysis update.

findings.finding: MI Link uses Always On availability group technology for near-real-time replication. Only truly online migration option — only downtime is instantaneous cutover. Can run for weeks/months before cutover. For 250GB, initial seeding takes hours but continuous replication means cutover is seconds. 5hr expected savings is conservative but reasonable.
findings.recommendation: No hours change needed. Rename AI alternative to 'MI Link (Managed Instance Link)' for clarity.

findings.finding: Playwright codegen records user interactions into test scripts. NEW: Playwright Test Agents explore apps and produce test plans via AI. MCP server integration with GitHub Copilot for AI-assisted test generation. 'Fix with AI' in VS Code extension. Limitations: doesn't handle complex logic, selectors may need manual review for dynamic Sitecore content.
findings.recommendation: 8hr expected savings plausible with Test Agents + codegen. Pessimistic 3hr floor adequate. No change needed.

findings.finding: Azure Cache for Redis is RETIRING across ALL tiers. Premium: creation blocked for new customers April 1, 2026, full retirement September 2028. Enterprise: creation blocked April 2026, retirement March 2027. Replacement: Azure Managed Redis (built on Redis Enterprise). Different pricing tiers. Sitecore session state should work (Redis protocol compatible) but explicit verification recommended.
findings.recommendation: Must use Azure Managed Redis for 2026 deployment. Update estimate, migration plan, and knowledge files. Verify Sitecore 10.4 session state compatibility.

summary: Estimate challenge review passed at 82%. 11 challenges (1 critical, 4 high, 6 medium). 7 resolved, 4 accepted. 0 open. Key findings: (1) SSL/TLS many_sites multiplier double-counts — unit count already reflects multi-site, recommend removing (+10hr to total); (2) 3 components hit 50% AI savings cap undisclosed; (3) MI Link confirmed for DB migration with 5hr savings; (4) MAJOR: Azure Cache for Redis retiring April 2026 — must use Azure Managed Redis; (5) Playwright Test Agents confirmed for AI test generation; (6) Hybrid AWS↔Azure connectivity risk gap documented.
